title: Electronics Engineer
description: An Electronics Engineer in India designs, develops, and tests electronic systems, circuits, and devices. They work on a wide range of projects, from consumer electronics to industrial control systems, and often collaborate with other engineers and technicians to ensure that their designs meet safety and performance standards. In their daily work, they may spend a significant amount of time analyzing data, writing code, and troubleshooting issues.
degree_name: Bachelor of Engineering in Electronics and Communication Engineering
top_private_colleges: Sri Sivasubramaniya Nadar College of Engineering

career_progression.year_0_2: Assist in designing and developing electronic systems, circuits, and devices under the guidance of senior engineers.
career_progression.year_3_5: Work on complex projects, lead small teams, and take ownership of design and development tasks.
career_progression.year_6_10: Lead large teams, manage projects, and make strategic decisions on technology and resource allocation.
career_progression.entrepreneurship_exit: Start a company focused on developing innovative electronic products or providing engineering services.
career_progression.global_pathway: Work in multinational companies, collaborate with international teams, or pursue higher education abroad.

india_social_context.first_year_reality: Starting salary is around ₹4-6 lakhs per annum, and the first year can be challenging due to the high expectations and workload.
common_misconceptions: Electronics Engineering is only about designing circuits.
Electronics Engineers only work in the IT industry.
